title: Deaths in armed conflicts based on where they occurred, 1989-2024
subtitle: Included are deaths of combatants and civilians due to fighting in [armed conflicts](#dod:armed-conflict-ucdp) that were ongoing over that time.
caption:
Deaths in armed conflicts based on where they occurred, 1989-2024
Included are deaths of combatants and civilians due to fighting in armed conflicts that were ongoing over that time.

This horizontal bar chart ranks total conflict fatalities by geographic region over a 35-year period, with the vertical axis listing six categories and the horizontal extent representing the cumulative death count. The data displays a steep hierarchy in the magnitude of violence across different parts of the globe. The top bar, labeled "World," aggregates the global total to 3.9 million deaths, serving as the reference sum for the regional breakdown below it. Africa occupies the second position with 2.0 million deaths, a figure that constitutes slightly more than half of the global total and stands as the highest value among the specific regions. The Middle East follows with 720,000 deaths, placing it in a distinct tier below Africa but well above the remaining areas. Asia and Oceania records 580,000 deaths, sitting just below the Middle East in the ranking. Europe accounts for 370,000 deaths, while the Americas shows the lowest count among the listed regions at 230,000 deaths. The visual gap between the top two entries and the rest of the list is substantial; the combined total for the Middle East, Asia and Oceania, Europe, and the Americas amounts to roughly 1.9 million, which is still less than the total recorded for Africa alone. The bars decrease in length from top to bottom, illustrating a distribution where one region bears a disproportionate share of the recorded fatalities compared to the others. The chart shows that conflict deaths are heavily concentrated in Africa and the Middle East relative to Europe and the Americas during this timeframe.
